senses.definition: to be able to use the information from a computer system, network, database, etc.

examples.x: Users need valid credentials to access the company's secure database.
After subscribing to the streaming service, members can access a vast library of movies and TV shows.
With the correct permissions, team members can access and edit files stored on the shared network drive.
The system requires users to provide a unique code to access confidential files.

senses.definition: a situation where a computer program or process stops working or does not work properly

examples.x: An error message appeared on the screen after the system failed.
He fixed the error by debugging the software.
The computer encountered an error while processing the data.
She received an error notification when the file couldn’t be uploaded.

senses.definition: one of many buttons that one presses or taps to operate a computer, telephone, or cell phone

examples.x: Each key on a keyboard or keypad corresponds to a specific character or function, enabling users to input data and control the device efficiently.
Touchscreen devices utilize virtual keys displayed on the screen, allowing users to interact by tapping or swiping to input text, execute commands, or navigate menus.
Keypads on telephones and cell phones feature numeric keys for dialing phone numbers, along with additional keys for functions like call control, messaging, and navigation.
The keyboard on a computer typically consists of alphanumeric keys for typing letters and numbers, along with specialized keys like Enter, Shift, and Backspace.
After pressing the key, the screen displayed the desired result.

senses.definition: to stop a connection to an online account or computer system by doing specific actions

examples.x: I need to log off my work computer before leaving for the day.
I always log off my social media accounts before closing my browser.
The individual logged off their personal computer to secure their privacy.

senses.definition: a set of coded instructions that control how a computer, cell phone, etc. works
the programs that a computer uses to perform specific tasks

examples.x: She installed new software to help design her website.
The company developed custom software to manage their inventory.
Updating your software regularly can improve your computer's performance.
The software allows users to edit videos with professional-level tools.
He uses accounting software to keep track of his business finances.

senses.definition: the most fundamental software that manages a computer, cell phone, etc., hardware and provides a platform for running applications

examples.x: The operating system controls how the computer runs and manages applications.
Windows and macOS are popular operating systems.
She prefers using an operating system with a simple interface.
Some operating systems are designed for mobile devices.
He switched from a Linux operating system to a Windows one.

senses.definition: to add a piece of software to a computer system

examples.x: I need to install the latest version of Microsoft Office on my computer for work.
The IT department will install antivirus software on all company laptops to enhance security.
Before you can use the new printer, you need to install the driver software on your computer.
The technician will install specialized accounting software to streamline financial processes.

senses.definition: the physical and electronic parts of a computer or other similar system

examples.x: The technician replaced the faulty hardware to fix the computer.
Upgrading the hardware improved the system's overall performance.
They installed new hardware to enhance the server's capabilities.
The hardware of her laptop included a high-resolution screen and a powerful processor.
He opened the computer case to examine the hardware inside.

senses.definition: the technology that allows computers, cell phones, etc. to access the Internet or exchange data wirelessly

examples.x: She connected her laptop to the Wi-Fi network to complete her work from the café.
The hotel offered free Wi-Fi to all its guests, making it easier to stay connected.
He upgraded his router to ensure a stronger Wi-Fi signal throughout the house.
They relied on public Wi-Fi while traveling to stay in touch with family and friends.
The new smartphone had excellent Wi-Fi capabilities, allowing for fast internet browsing.

senses.definition: to close a computer program

examples.x: Don’t forget to quit the app before turning off your computer.
She accidentally quit the game before saving her progress.
After finishing the report, he remembered to quit the word processor.
Make sure to quit all open programs to free up memory.
She clicked the "X" to quit the program and shut down her laptop.

senses.definition: computer programs and pieces of hardware operating together
the action of pressing a button on a computer mouse
a small area or part of a machine or electronic device, which when pressed starts working

examples.x: Press the button to turn on the television.
The elevator has a button for each floor.
She pushed the red button to start the machine.
The car's engine started when he pressed the start button.
The button on the remote control was stuck.
She touched the button on the phone screen to answer the call.

senses.definition: to write using a physical or digital keyboard

examples.x: She typed her essay on her laptop.
The secretary typed the meeting minutes on her desktop computer.
The novelist typed furiously to meet the deadline for her manuscript.
The student typed notes during the lecture using a tablet.

senses.definition: an area on a computer screen that looks like a frame displaying the operation of a specific program

examples.x: She opened a new window to check her email while browsing the web.
You can resize the window by dragging its corners.
He minimized the window to make more room for the spreadsheet.
The program crashed, and the error message appeared in a separate window.

senses.definition: able to operate without wires

examples.x: The wireless headphones allow you to move freely without being tethered to your device.
The office installed a wireless network to improve connectivity for all employees.
The wireless mouse is more convenient than the traditional wired one.
The wireless security cameras provide real-time monitoring without the need for extensive wiring.

senses.definition: to join a device such as a computer or cell phone to a computer network or the Internet

examples.x: After entering the correct password, I was able to connect my laptop to the Wi-Fi network.
I can connect my tablet to the Internet through the available public Wi-Fi at the café.
To print documents wirelessly, you need to connect your printer to the home Wi-Fi network.
The gaming console automatically connects to the online gaming platform when powered on.
The new fitness tracker seamlessly connects to your smartphone to sync health data.

senses.definition: the act of establishing or the state of being linked to something

examples.x: Despite numerous attempts, John still couldn't establish a stable connection to the Wi-Fi network in his apartment.
Lisa's new smartphone comes with lightning-fast internet connection, allowing her to stream videos and browse the web effortlessly.
The faulty ethernet cable was replaced, restoring the connection to the office network and enabling employees to resume their work.
The connection between the satellite dish and the television receiver was disrupted during the storm, causing a temporary loss of signal.

senses.definition: information that a computer can use or store

examples.x: She entered the customer data into the database for future reference.
The app collects data on user activity to improve its features.
The company's servers store terabytes of data, ensuring rapid access for their web applications.
The data from users' smartphones is used to improve the accuracy of map applications.
Streaming platforms use data to recommend personalized content to their users.

senses.definition: a place on a computer system that holds files or other pieces of data together
to remove a piece of data from a computer or smartphone

examples.x: She decided to delete the old files from her computer to free up space.
He accidentally hit the wrong button and managed to delete all his contacts.
The app allows users to delete messages they no longer need.
Make sure to delete your browsing history for privacy reasons.
He had to delete the unnecessary apps to make room for the update.

senses.definition: to move a piece of computer data, such as an image, across a computer or smartphone screen using the mouse or a finger

examples.x: To rearrange the icons on the desktop, simply click and drag them to your desired positions.
She decided to drag the image from the web browser directly into her presentation slides.
When organizing your email inbox, you can drag emails into different folders for better categorization.
To attach a document to your email, you can simply drag the file from your desktop into the email composition window.
In spreadsheet programs, users frequently drag cells or columns to reorganize data.

senses.definition: to move what is being displayed on a computer or smartphone screen up or down to see different parts of it

examples.x: He scrolled down the webpage to read the full article.
The user scrolled up to review previous messages in the chat.
The customer scrolled through product listings on the online store.
He scrolled down the document to find the information he needed.
She scrolled through her social media feed to catch up on the latest news.

senses.definition: to make a copy of computer digital data

examples.x: I'll back up my important documents before the software update.
Make it a habit to regularly back up your important folders.
The software prompts users to back up their information regularly.
Remember to back up your important files regularly.

senses.definition: an area on a computer where the icons of programs are displayed

examples.x: The file shortcut is saved on the desktop.
She arranged her desktop icons for better organization.
The desktop background was a picture of a beach.
I moved the document to the desktop for quick access.
His desktop was cluttered with too many icons.

senses.definition: a different form of something particular when compared with its previous form or forms

examples.x: The software company released a new version of its popular app with enhanced features.
This is an updated version of the report that includes recent data.
He prefers the original version of the song over the remix.
The director's cut is considered the definitive version of the film.
They are working on a digital version of the classic board game for modern audiences.

senses.definition: a harmful program capable of multiplying itself and corrupting files and not allowing a system, like that of a computer, to function properly

examples.x: The virus damaged important system files.
He installed antivirus software to remove the virus.
A dangerous virus spread through email attachments.
The virus slowed down the entire computer network.
Hackers used a virus to steal personal data.
